stories.title: Create product definition document
acceptance: docs/product.md exists in the repository
Document includes problem statement, vision, and MVP scope
Document includes a clear user workflow section

stories.title: Create machine-readable backlog
acceptance: docs/backlog.json exists in the repository
Backlog entries are valid JSON objects in a stories array
JSON parses successfully with a standard validator

stories.title: Record architectural decisions
acceptance: docs/decisions.md exists in the repository
At least one architecture decision is documented with rationale
Decision entries are readable and version-controlled

stories.title: Establish Delay Repay assistant as MVP product direction
acceptance: docs/product.md states that the product is a Delay Repay assistant rather than a journey planner
Product scope prioritises station departures search, service selection, delay derivation, and operator claim handoff
Journey planner is documented as a future enhancement
Product roadmap reflects Phase A (departures-based MVP), Phase B (destination verification via service timetable), and Phase C (arrival-based delay using live running data such as Darwin)

stories.title: Introduce Journey + Leg domain model
acceptance: Journey type supports one or more ordered legs
Journey delayMins is computed from final planned arrival versus final expected arrival
Shared journey/leg types are imported by both API and UI modules
Existing search page renders without runtime errors after type migration

stories.title: Define stable Journey API contract (v1)
acceptance: /api/journeys returns JSON for both success and error paths
Successful response includes a top-level journeys array
Validation errors return HTTP 400 with a structured error body
Responses do not include secrets or credentials

stories.title: Integrate TransportAPI journey planner endpoint
acceptance: Journey planner integration returns multi-leg journeys when upstream data provides them
Upstream journey legs are mapped into the internal Journey + Leg model
If upstream planning fails, API returns a controlled error JSON response

stories.title: Timetable fallback for non-now queries (avoid empty results)
acceptance: When date is not today, /api/journeys uses a timetable-capable feed (not station live) and returns services around the requested time
When date is today and requested time is within ±180 minutes of now, /api/journeys uses station live feed
When date is today but requested time is outside ±180 minutes of now, /api/journeys uses timetable feed
API response shape remains unchanged (query, services, source, note) and service field names remain consistent
Time-window filtering is applied consistently for both sources using expectedDeparture ?? aimedDeparture as the canonical filter time
Response source is transportapi.station_live or transportapi.station_timetable depending on chosen feed
Dev-only logs include chosenSource, requested date/time/window, and beforeCount/afterCount
scripts/validate.mjs includes deterministic checks for source selection (non-today → timetable; today-but-far-from-now → timetable)

stories.title: Fix journey/service summary fields (dep/arr) to reflect whole journey or selected leg
acceptance: Top-line card shows departure time from the first leg (or selected leg if user picked one)
Top-line card shows arrival time from the final leg (or selected leg)
No bogus fallback where dep==arr when arrival is unknown
If arrival is unavailable, UI shows "—" and status is not forced to "On time"

stories.title: Derive service status from planned vs expected times
acceptance: Delay minutes are calculated from expected arrival minus aimed arrival
If delay minutes > 0 the service status renders as "Delayed"
If expected time equals aimed time status renders as "On time"
If expected time is missing status renders as "Unknown"
Cancelled services render status "Cancelled"
ServiceCard status pill updates correctly from the derived status

stories.title: Switch /api/journeys to TransportAPI Journey Planner (from -> to)
acceptance: /api/journeys calls Journey Planner endpoint with from, to, date, time, service=train, live=true
API maps journey-planner response into the existing services array contract
API returns JSON on success and upstream error paths
UI renders distinct services for SEV -> LBG search
stories.notes: Awaiting manual smoke test in local dev UI before marking done.

stories.title: Fetch service timetable on card expand to confirm destination calling point
acceptance: When a ServiceCard expands, the client fetches the service_timetable.id URL from TransportAPI
The response includes the list of calling stations for the service
The expanded card shows calling points
The app determines whether the train stops at the selected destination
If the timetable fetch fails, the card still renders without crashing
Timetable requests only occur when a card is expanded

stories.title: Make time-window filtering resilient (avoid empty results + explain when empty)
acceptance: If upstream returns services but none fall within ±windowMins, API returns the nearest N services (e.g. 3) outside the window, plus a flag explaining they are outside the requested window
API response includes a field like `windowApplied: true` and `windowMatchedCount`
UI shows a clear message when results are outside the requested window (e.g. “No trains in the requested window; showing nearest services”)
If upstream truly returns no services, UI shows the existing “No services found” message
docs/backlog.json remains valid JSON

stories.title: Service cards + expandable details drawer
acceptance: Results render as service cards (no horizontal scrolling)
Each service supports expand/collapse drawer
Collapsed view shows: depart time, arrive time (if known), operator, platform (if known), status
Expanded drawer shows: calling points (when available), UID/service id, aimed vs expected times, delay mins
Mobile layout remains readable

stories.title: Graceful offline + network error handling
acceptance: If the browser is offline or the fetch fails, the UI shows a friendly message (no red dev overlay in production).
Show a clear Retry button that re-runs the search without losing the current form/URL state.
If the API returns a non-JSON response or 5xx, show a user-friendly error message (no stack trace exposure).
If the API returns 400 validation errors, show the specific validation message to the user.
Errors are rendered in the existing error banner area (no layout break, no console spam required).

stories.title: Replace status default 'On time' with 'Unknown' when realtime fields missing
acceptance: If expected times/status cannot be derived, status renders as "Unknown"
"On time" is only shown when data supports it (e.g., explicit on-time or expected==aimed)
ServiceCard renders gracefully for missing fields without crashing

stories.title: Station search typeahead using full UK station list
acceptance: App includes a dataset containing all UK rail stations (CRS code and station name)
User can type a station name or CRS code into the search box
Matching stations appear as a typeahead dropdown
Selecting a station populates the CRS code used by the API
The dataset loads once and is cached client-side
The search works for all UK stations

stories.title: Auto-detect nearest station using browser geolocation
acceptance: App requests browser geolocation permission
If granted, determine the nearest station from the station dataset
Automatically populate the From field with that station
If permission denied, user continues with manual station search
Geolocation runs once per session
No location data is stored server-side

stories.title: Graceful offline and upstream outage handling in search UI
acceptance: If the browser is offline, UI shows a clear 'You appear to be offline' message before calling /api/journeys
If /api/journeys returns a network error or times out, UI shows a friendly retry state (no crash, no blank screen)
If /api/journeys returns a JSON error body, UI displays the message safely (no HTML injection) and keeps the form usable
A 'Retry' action re-runs the last search without retyping inputs
Errors are shown inline near results area and do not break StationTypeahead
No stack traces or raw HTML are shown to the user

stories.title: Multi-leg journeys + choose delayed leg (or whole journey)
acceptance: Journey supports 1+ legs in UI rendering
User can choose 'whole journey' or a specific leg as the delayed segment
Selection is persisted in URL params (shareable)
Chosen segment drives delay mins shown for Delay Repay handoff

stories.title: Delay Repay handoff by operator + copy-pack
acceptance: Operator is displayed for the selected segment/service
User sees a 'Claim Delay Repay' button that opens the correct operator claim page
UI provides a copy-pack of key fields: from/to, date, planned times, expected times (if available), delay mins, service UID
If operator mapping is missing, UI shows a clear fallback path (generic guidance) without breaking

stories.title: Integrate live rail running data
acceptance: API uses live rail provider data instead of mock journey data
Delayed and cancelled services are represented in API response fields
Upstream errors are returned as controlled JSON without server stack details

stories.title: Integrate Darwin real-time feed behind feature flag (live + fixture modes)
acceptance: /api/journeys supports selecting data source via an env flag (e.g. DARWIN_MODE=off|fixture|live)
When DARWIN_MODE=fixture, the API returns deterministic services from local fixture files (no network calls)
When DARWIN_MODE=live, the API fetches Darwin data and maps it into the existing service model used by the UI
Cancelled services are represented consistently (status=Cancelled) and do not show misleading delay mins
Delay minutes are derived from expected vs aimed times (arrival preferred when available)
Time-window filtering is applied consistently using expectedDeparture ?? aimedDeparture as the canonical filter time
Upstream errors return HTTP 502 with a safe JSON body (no secrets, no raw HTML)
scripts/validate.mjs includes a deterministic regression check that fails if out-of-window services appear
stories.notes: Pivot: TransportAPI station/live and timetable feeds are not reliable for historical Delay Repay scenarios; Darwin is the source of truth.

stories.title: Calculate Delay Repay eligibility
acceptance: Delay minutes are calculated from final journey arrival delta
Eligibility threshold rules produce a deterministic eligibility state
Cancelled journeys are marked eligible according to configured rules

stories.title: Journey cards with expandable details
acceptance: Legacy implementation plan is reconciled with UI-001 card and drawer design
No duplicate card components remain after consolidation
Journey card interactions are covered by component-level tests
stories.notes: Superseded by UI-001. Do not implement separately.

stories.title: Operator Delay Repay handoff
acceptance: Legacy handoff flow is merged into DR-002 without conflicting UI actions
Operator handoff uses a single mapping source of truth
Regression tests verify claim-link behavior for mapped and unmapped operators
stories.notes: Superseded by DR-002. Do not implement separately.

stories.title: Service cards with expandable details drawer
acceptance: Backlog alignment confirms UI-001 is the canonical implementation story
Any duplicate implementation tasks are linked to UI-001 and not executed independently
UI behavior and acceptance for cards/drawers matches UI-001 criteria
stories.notes: Superseded by UI-001. Do not implement separately.

stories.title: Multi-leg journey capture and delayed-leg selection
acceptance: Backlog alignment confirms DR-001 is the canonical multi-leg selection story
No conflicting delayed-segment state model exists outside DR-001
Links and references point engineering work to DR-001 acceptance criteria
stories.notes: Superseded by DR-001. Do not implement separately.

stories.title: Delay Repay handoff by operator with copy-pack
acceptance: Backlog alignment confirms DR-002 is the canonical operator handoff story
No duplicate copy-pack payload definitions remain outside DR-002
All follow-up work references DR-002 as implementation source
stories.notes: Superseded by DR-002. Do not implement separately.

stories.title: Map cancelled / no-report states into service model and display
acceptance: Internal status model supports Cancelled and No report / Not reporting (or equivalent)
UI displays Cancelled clearly and does not show delay mins for cancelled unless rules define it
API mapping sets the correct status when upstream indicates cancellation or no-report
Copy-details includes status text for the selected service/leg

stories.title: Validate and normalise all user inputs
acceptance: Station inputs are validated server-side before provider requests
Date/time inputs are normalized and invalid values return HTTP 400
Malformed queries do not reach upstream providers
API never trusts client input for security-sensitive fields

stories.title: Prevent cross-site scripting (XSS)
acceptance: No dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage is present in the application code
Untrusted external data is rendered as escaped text
Automated test cases verify injected script strings do not execute

stories.title: Add rate limiting to API routes
acceptance: Requests are limited per client IP within a configured window
Exceeded limits return HTTP 429 with JSON error payload
Rate limit thresholds are configurable via environment variables

stories.title: Keep API secrets server-side only
acceptance: Transport API credentials are loaded only from server-side environment variables
No secret values are present in client bundles or API responses
External provider calls are executed only on the server

stories.title: Add baseline security headers
acceptance: Responses include Content-Security-Policy header
Responses include X-Frame-Options: DENY
Responses include X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Responses include a strict referrer policy

stories.title: Validate outbound operator links
acceptance: Operator claim URLs are resolved from an allowlisted mapping
User-controlled input cannot alter redirect destination
Unmapped operators return a safe fallback guidance route

stories.title: Safe request logging without PII
acceptance: Error and request logs exclude personal data fields
Structured logs include timestamp, route, and status code
Logging can be disabled or redacted via configuration

stories.title: Run MVP red-team checklist
acceptance: Checklist includes XSS, API abuse, secret exposure, and link-manipulation scenarios
Each scenario has a pass/fail result recorded in docs/security.md
All high-severity findings are resolved or tracked with explicit follow-up stories

stories.title: Hide stack traces in production API responses
acceptance: Stack traces are returned only when NODE_ENV is development
Production API error responses do not expose internal stack details
Error responses remain valid JSON in all environments

stories.title: Validate station CRS codes server-side in journeys API
acceptance: Only CRS codes present in the known station index are accepted
Unknown or malformed station codes return HTTP 400
Server-side validation runs regardless of client-side checks

stories.title: Implement server-side input validation for date and time
acceptance: Date input must match YYYY-MM-DD format
Time input must match HH:MM 24-hour format
Invalid date or time values return HTTP 400
Validation is enforced server-side without relying on client validation

stories.title: User accounts for saved journeys
acceptance: Authenticated user can save a journey from search results
Saved journeys are listed on a dedicated user view
User can delete a saved journey and list updates immediately

stories.title: Delay alerts for saved journeys
acceptance: User can enable or disable delay alerts per saved journey
Alert event is generated when delay threshold is exceeded
User receives alert payload containing journey and delay summary

stories.title: Codex commit helper (safe mode: no auto-commit until tests pass)
acceptance: Codex can stage specific files using git add
Codex can generate a proposed commit message based on staged diff
Codex runs validation commands before suggesting commit (npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build)
Codex will NOT run git commit unless validation passes and the user explicitly confirms
Codex never stages or commits .env or secret files
Validation results are displayed clearly before commit suggestion

stories.title: Auto-close backlog stories from commit messages
acceptance: Commit messages referencing story IDs are detected
Referenced stories are automatically marked done in docs/backlog.json
completedAt is set automatically
Backlog remains valid JSON after update

stories.title: Add fixture-based validation for journeys time filtering and sorting
acceptance: A fixture file is added under scripts/fixtures/ (or equivalent) with representative services (aimed/expected/cancelled/unknown)
scripts/validate.mjs runs deterministic assertions for: filtering, expected-over-aimed behaviour, and sorting
Validation fails with a clear message when behaviour regresses
npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, and npm run build remain green

stories.title: Replace local station dataset with national station index
acceptance: Application supports searching all UK stations
Station typeahead queries national station dataset
Dataset includes CRS code and station name
Existing station typeahead UI continues to function

stories.title: Introduce CI/CD pipeline for build and deployment
acceptance: GitHub Actions pipeline runs on push
Pipeline runs lint, typecheck, and build
Successful builds trigger deployment
Failed builds block deployment
